name: Google Shape;64;p14
text:
WorkDNA is an offline recruiter-style evidence engine that ranks candidates by proven fit, not keyword overlap.

The new JD Match flow closes the exact challenge loop: paste a job description, extract role missions, and rerank candidates live.

The system separates three recruiter questions:
• WorkDNA: Has this candidate actually done work similar to the role?
• Skill Evidence Ratio: Are claimed skills backed by projects, assessments, duration, or endorsements?
• Career Physics: Is the candidate growing, recovering, or plateauing as complexity increases?

Differentiator: most systems stop at semantic similarity. We model JD needs + fit + proof + movement, then explain every shortlist decision.
textPreview: WorkDNA is an offline recruiter-style evidence engine that ranks candidates by proven fit, not keyword overlap. |  | The new JD Match flo...

name: Google Shape;71;p15
text:
The JD is converted into five success missions instead of a bag of words:
• Production: shipped/deployed systems, real users, monitoring, operational depth
• Retrieval: search, ranking, recommendation, RAG, vectors, relevance
• Evaluation: labels, offline metrics, A/B tests, human judgments, benchmark discipline
• Ownership: owned/led/designed/architected vs only assisted
• Transferability: whether project patterns map to this hiring problem even when exact words differ

Candidate fit uses career history, project narratives, skill proof, assessments, recruiter activity, response behavior, availability, and contradiction flags.
textPreview: The JD is converted into five success missions instead of a bag of words: | • Production: shipped/deployed systems, real users, monitorin...

name: Google Shape;78;p16
text:
Ranking stack:
1. Parse profiles into project/career evidence and stable project archetypes.
2. Score WorkDNA using project evidence, production proof, evaluation maturity, ownership, transferability, availability, and contradiction penalties.
3. Compute Skill Evidence Ratio = supported skills / claimed skills. Final skill score = 65% skill proof + 35% WorkDNA.
4. Compute Career Physics = 35% current complexity + 35% velocity percentile + 20% acceleration percentile + 10% recovery percentile.
5. Sort candidates by the selected model and expose the evidence behind the rank.

No external API is required for ranking; the system runs offline on the actual dataset.
textPreview: Ranking stack: | 1. Parse profiles into project/career evidence and stable project archetypes. | 2. Score WorkDNA using project evidence,...

name: Google Shape;85;p17
text:
Every candidate receives both numbers and recruiter-readable reasoning:
• Mission bars: Production, Retrieval, Evaluation, Ownership, Transferability
• Skill Evidence: supported vs unsupported skills with evidence source
• Career Physics: timeline, velocity, acceleration, recovery, five-year bounded projection
• Strict audit: skill proof, project breadth, narrative specificity, project reliance, context independence, credibility

Hallucination guardrail: explanations are generated only from fields present in the dataset. Unsupported skills are penalized; contradictions, no production evidence, weak narrative specificity, and low proof trigger hard caps/manual-review warnings.
textPreview: Every candidate receives both numbers and recruiter-readable reasoning: | • Mission bars: Production, Retrieval, Evaluation, Ownership, T...

name: Google Shape;92;p18
text:
Workflow from JD to trusted shortlist:
1. Recruiter pastes a target job description in JD Match.
2. System extracts mission weights: production, retrieval, evaluation, ownership, transferability.
3. Dataset is loaded/imported locally; original data remains untouched.
4. Candidate career history, projects, skills, activity, and availability are transformed into evidence features.
5. JD Match reranks candidates using extracted JD weights plus WorkDNA, Skill Evidence, and Career Physics.
6. UI shows ranked candidates, extracted JD signals, candidate-vs-median comparison, model explanation, and interview calibration.
7. Recruiter exports the ranked output CSV for submission or hiring workflow.

name: Google Shape;105;p20
text:
Quality sanity checks from the actual dataset:
• Best WorkDNA fit: CAND_0064326 / Nisha Pillai — real search/ranking/RAG/evaluation evidence; WorkDNA 100.00.
• Worst role fit: CAND_0056983 / Arnav Mittal — unsupported technical skills, contradictions, no AI/retrieval proof; WorkDNA 0.00.
• Nuance case: CAND_0007412 / Pranav Shah — strong WorkDNA and Skill Evidence, but Career Physics flags declining complexity.
• Top Skill Evidence: CAND_0036863 / Vikram Bansal — Skill Evidence 98.33; claims are heavily corroborated.
• Top Career Physics: CAND_0008425 / Myra Krishnan — Career Physics 95.17; strongest growth/trajectory pattern.

Runtime/compute: CPU-only, offline, no external inference dependency, supports local import/export and works on the full challenge dataset.

name: Google Shape;112;p21
text:
Frontend: React + Vite for a fast offline dashboard and professional demo UI.
Backend: Python local server for dataset loading, model scoring, export, and static frontend serving.
Data/modeling: JSONL/CSV processing, engineered evidence features, pairwise WorkDNA ranking, skill-proof ratio, career-complexity time series, strict evidence audit, population median benchmarks.
Visualization: candidate ledger, model tabs, radar/mission-style metrics, career timeline forecast, candidate-vs-median hover comparison, Explain tab with sanity checks.
Why this stack: reliable offline demo, no API failure risk, transparent scoring, easy CSV submission, simple hosting path.

name: Google Shape;119;p22
text:
Submission assets checklist:
• GitHub repository: [Fill GitHub repo link]
• Hosted/live demo: [Fill deployed URL or local demo note]
• Ranked output file: export from app using selected model tab
• Deck/PDF: this presentation converted to PDF
• Demo command: cd "ML Project" && .\START_WORKDNA.ps1
• Local app URL: http://127.0.0.1:8765

Judge demo flow:
1. Open JD Match and paste/show the job description extraction.
2. Show extracted mission weights and reranked candidate ledger.
3. Open Explain tab and show the 60-second story.
4. Show sanity check: where keyword search gets it wrong.
5. Open WorkDNA top candidate and hover candidate ID for median comparison.
6. Open Skill Evidence and Career Physics.
7. Export ranked CSV.
